title: IoT Sensor Monitoring
x-sia-instruction: IoT device and sensor data. Device ID and last reading are essential. Alert status must always be surfaced prominently.

Device.x-sia-instruction: An IoT device. Always show device name, status, and last seen time. If status is 'offline' or 'error', highlight immediately.

deviceId.x-sia-instruction: Unique hardware identifier. Use for technical references. Display the friendlyName for users.

friendlyName.x-sia-instruction: Human-readable device name. e.g., 'Warehouse Temp Sensor #3'. Always prefer this over deviceId in UI.

type.x-sia-instruction: Sensor type determines which readings and thresholds are relevant.

location.x-sia-instruction: Physical location. Format as 'Building > Floor > Zone' for breadcrumb display.

status.x-sia-instruction: CRITICAL: 'error' and 'offline' states must be flagged visually. 'decommissioned' devices should be hidden from active dashboards.

lastSeen.x-sia-instruction: Last communication timestamp. If older than the device's expected interval, flag as potentially offline.

latestReading.x-sia-instruction: Most recent sensor measurement. Always show with the device in dashboards.

thresholds.x-sia-instruction: Alert thresholds. Use to determine if latest reading is in normal, warning, or critical range.

firmware.x-sia-instruction: Firmware version. Only relevant for maintenance and update operations.

batteryPercent.x-sia-instruction: Battery level. Null for mains-powered devices. Flag if below 20%.

tags.x-sia-instruction: Organisational tags for grouping and filtering. e.g., 'cold-chain', 'critical-infrastructure'.

SensorReading.x-sia-instruction: A single sensor measurement at a point in time. Value and unit must always appear together. Timestamp is essential.

value.x-sia-instruction: The measured value. Always display with the unit. Round to appropriate precision for the sensor type.

timestamp.x-sia-instruction: When this measurement was taken. Display as relative time for recent readings ('2 min ago'), absolute for older.

quality.x-sia-instruction: Signal/measurement quality. 'degraded' and 'poor' readings should be visually marked as unreliable.

ThresholdConfig.x-sia-instruction: Alert thresholds for a device. Defines normal, warning, and critical ranges.

criticalLow.x-sia-instruction: Below this value triggers critical alert. Requires immediate attention.

criticalHigh.x-sia-instruction: Above this value triggers critical alert. Requires immediate attention.

Alert.x-sia-instruction: An alert triggered by a sensor reading exceeding thresholds. Severity and device are always essential. Never downplay critical alerts.

device.x-sia-instruction: The device that triggered this alert. Show device name and location.

severity.x-sia-instruction: Alert severity. Critical alerts should be styled with red, warnings with amber, info with blue.

message.x-sia-instruction: Human-readable alert description. e.g., 'Temperature exceeded 35°C threshold'.

reading.x-sia-instruction: The reading that triggered the alert.

acknowledged.x-sia-instruction: Whether a human has acknowledged this alert. Unacknowledged critical alerts should be escalated.

resolvedAt.x-sia-instruction: Null = still active. Non-null = resolved. Show duration between triggeredAt and resolvedAt.
